title: Naan Aanaiyittal Lyrics Enga Veettu Pillai
stanzas: <div>Naan Aanaiyittal Lyrics Enga Veettu Pillai

Naan aanaiyittaal
adhu nadanthuvittaal

[Naan aanaiyittaal
adhu nadanthuvittaal
ingu yezhaigal
vedhanai padamaataar

uyir ullavarai
oru thunbam illai
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar] (2)

Oru thavaru seithaal
athai therindhu seithaal
avan dhevan endraalum
vidamaatten(2)

udal uzhaikka solven
adhil pizhaika solven
avar urimai porugalai
thodamaatten.

Naan aanaiyittaal
adhu nadanthuvittaal
ingu yezhaigal
vedhanai padamaataar

uyir ullavarai
oru thunbam illai
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar

silar aasaikkum thaevaikkum
vazhvukkum vasadhikkum
ooraar kaal pidippaar

oru maanam
adhil eenam illai
avar eppodhum kaal pidippar

edhirkaalam varum
en kadamai varum
indha koottathil aattathai
olippen.

podhu needhiyile
pudhu paadhaiyile
varum nallor
mugathile vizhippen.
varum nallor
mugathile vizhippen.

Naan aanaiyittaal
adhu nadanthuvittaal
ingu yezhaigal
vedhanai padamaataar

uyir ullavarai
oru thunbam illai
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar

ingu oomaigal yaengavum
unmaigal thoongavum
naanaa paarthiruppen

oru kadavul undu
avan kolgai undu
adhai eppodhum
kaathiruppen.

munbu Yeasu vandhaar
pinbu Gandhi vandhaar
indha maanidar thirundhida
pirandhaar

ivar thirundhavillai
manam varundhavillai
andha maelor sonnadhai
marandhaar
andha maelor sonnadhai
marandhaar

Naan aanaiyittaal
adhu nadanthuvittaal
ingu yezhaigal
vedhanai padamaataar

uyir ullavarai
oru thunbam illai
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar
avar kanneer kadalile
vilamaattaar

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Naan Aanaiyittal'?

The song expresses deep emotions of longing and the pain of separation, reflecting the sentiments of the characters in the movie.

What is the plot context of the movie 'Enga Veettu Pillai'?

The movie revolves around family dynamics and the struggles faced by the protagonist, which is beautifully captured in this song.

Who is the singer of 'Naan Aanaiyittal'?

The song is sung by the legendary TMS, known for his emotive singing style.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for 'Naan Aanaiyittal' was composed by the iconic duo Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy.

When was 'Naan Aanaiyittal' released?

The song was released in 1970, making it a classic in Tamil music history.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the song Naan Aanaiyittal from Enga Veettu Pillai, the protagonist expresses profound feelings of longing and sorrow. The lyrics beautifully convey the pain of separation, resonating with anyone who has experienced heartbreak. The soulful melody, combined with TMS's emotive voice, evokes a sense of nostalgia and reflection. This classic piece remains a cherished gem in Tamil cinema, capturing the essence of human emotions.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Naan Aanaiyittal from Enga Veettu Pillai is a timeless classic that resonates with deep emotions and nostalgia. The soulful rendition captures the essence of longing and heartache, making it a memorable piece in Tamil cinema. Experience the magic of TMS's voice intertwined with the enchanting melodies of Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy.
stanzaMeanings: If I command, it will happen.
If I command, it will happen; here, the cries of pain will not be heard.
Those who have life do not feel any sorrow; their tears will not flow in the sea.
If a mistake is made and it is acknowledged, even if he is a god, he will not escape.
I will speak about the body’s work; I will not let it be flawed; I will not let them take away their rights.
If I command, it will happen; here, the cries of pain will not be heard.
Those who have life do not feel any sorrow; their tears will not flow in the sea.
Some people hold on to desires, needs, and life; they hold on to the world.
There is no dignity in it; they always hold on to it.
The time of reckoning will come; my duty will come; I will reveal this in the group.
In public governance, in new paths, I will see the faces of good people that come.
I will see the faces of good people that come.
If I command, it will happen; here, the cries of pain will not be heard.
Those who have life do not feel any sorrow; their tears will not flow in the sea.
Here, I have seen both lies and truths.
There is a god; he has a form; I will always wait for that.
Before, Jesus came; later, Gandhi came; these people were born to change humanity.
They have not changed; they do not feel regret; they have forgotten what that leader said.
If I command, it will happen; here, the cries of pain will not be heard.
Those who have life do not feel any sorrow; their tears will not flow in the sea.
